text: Self-correction loops are a design pattern where Claude runs, collects feedback via a goal or rubric, self-corrects, and proceeds until the goal or rubric is satisfied.

evidence.quote: This let’s Claude run, collect feedback via the goal or rubric, self-correct, and proceed until the goal or rubric is satisfied.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Parameter Golf is an open source ML engineering challenge to train the best model that fits in a 16MB artifact in under 10 minutes on 8xH100s.

evidence.quote: Parameter Golf is an open source ML engineering challenge to train the best model that fits in a 16MB artifact in < 10 minutes on 8xH100s.

text: Outcomes is a feature in Claude Managed Agents that spawns a grader sub-agent to handle independent grading in a separate context window.

evidence.quote: Outcomes in CMA handles this by spawning a grader sub-agent for you.

text: A verifier sub-agent tends to outperform self-critique with Fable 5, because grading is done in an independent context window.

evidence.quote: We’ve found that a verifier sub-agent tends to outperform self-critique with Fable 5, because grading is done in an independent context window.

text: Fable 5 improved the training pipeline 6x more than Opus 4.7 on the Parameter Golf challenge.

evidence.quote: Fable 5 improved the training pipeline 6x more than Opus 4.7.

text: Opus 4.7's first experiment produced a small win and nearly everything after followed the same template: adjust a scalar, measure, keep if positive.

evidence.quote: Opus 4.7's first experiment produced a small win and nearly everything after followed the same template: adjust a scalar, measure, keep if positive.

text: Fable 5 bet on larger structural changes and showed resilience, such as pushing through a quantization regression to its biggest win.

evidence.quote: Fable 5 bet on larger structural changes and showed resilience (e.g., pushing through a quantization regression to its biggest win).

text: Memory can be thought of as an outer loop that spans across sessions: Claude writes to memory during a session and those memories can be retrieved in future sessions.

evidence.quote: We can think about this as a outer loop that spans across sessions: Claude writes to memory during a session and those memories can be retrieved in future sessions.

text: Effective use of memory benefits from a progression: fail (get something wrong and document), investigate (figure out why), verify (turn diagnosis into a checked fact), distill (turn verification into a general rule), and consult (read the rule instead of re-deriving it).

evidence.quote: fail (get something wrong and document), investigate (before moving on, figure out why), verify (turn the diagnosis into a checked fact), distill (turn verification into a general rule), and consult (read the rule, instead of re-deriving it).

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: To improve performance, task-specific memory instructions are needed.

evidence.quote: To improve performance, task-specific memory instructions are needed.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Fable 5 tends to complete the memory progression; in its strongest runs, verification coverage is up to 73% (22 of 30) and it distills learnings into general rules that help with future tasks.

evidence.quote: Fable 5 tends to complete the progression: in its strongest runs, verification coverage is up to 73% (22 of 30) and it distills learnings into general rules that help with future tasks.

text: Rather than directly prompting and steering Fable 5, it is often better to design loops that let the model self-correct in response to environment feedback and manage its own context via memory.

evidence.quote: Rather than directly prompting and steering Fable 5, it's often better to design loops that let the model to self-correct in response to environment feedback (e.g., /goal or Outcomes) and manage its own context (e.g., via memory).

text: We've seen that models have problems with self-critique on their own outputs.

evidence.quote: We've seen that models have problems with self-critique on their own outputs.

text: Rather than directly prompting and steering Fable 5, it's often better to design loops that let the model to self-correct in response to environment feedback (e.g., /goal or Outcomes) and manage its own context (e.g., via memory).

evidence.quote: Rather than directly prompting and steering Fable 5, it's often better to design loops that let the model to self-correct in response to environment feedback (e.g., /goal or Outcomes) and manage its own context (e.g., via memory).

text: Benchmarks today assume models are stateless.

evidence.quote: Benchmarks today assume models are stateless.
